program_context.title: Youth Employment and SME Skills Pathways
program_context.problem_summary: Youth face high unemployment and mismatch between labor-market needs and practical skills, especially for women and vulnerable young people outside major urban centers.
program_context.target_population: Youth aged 18-29, with priority for women and low-income participants
assumptions: Labor demand remains sufficient in target sectors (digital services, light manufacturing, tourism services).
Private-sector partners maintain internship and placement commitments.

theory_of_change.if_then: If youth receive market-relevant technical and employability training, and SMEs engage in co-designed internship and placement pathways, then youth employment and retention will increase, including for women and underserved groups.
key_assumptions: Training quality remains aligned with employer demand.
Participant barriers (transport/childcare) are partially mitigated by program support.

inputs: EU funding, training providers, employer network, placement coordinators, support services budget
activities: SME internship agreements and retention mentoring
outputs: Career plans and job-readiness portfolios completed
outcomes: Higher placement rate for graduates within 6 months
Improved median earnings among employed graduates
logic_model.impact: Reduced youth unemployment pressure and improved inclusive economic participation over 3-5 years.

O1_placement.name: Share of women graduates placed within 6 months
O1_placement.definition_formula: (women graduates placed within 6 months) / women graduates * 100

O1_placement.name: SME satisfaction with graduate job readiness
O1_placement.definition_formula: (SMEs rating graduates >= 4 on 5-point readiness scale) / responding SMEs * 100

O2_retention_income.name: 12-month employment retention rate
O2_retention_income.definition_formula: (graduates employed at 12 months after placement) / graduates placed * 100

O2_retention_income.name: Median earnings change among employed graduates
O2_retention_income.definition_formula: ((median monthly earnings at follow-up - baseline) / baseline) * 100

O2_retention_income.name: Share of graduates employed in trained occupation
O2_retention_income.definition_formula: (graduates employed in field aligned with training) / employed graduates * 100
O2_retention_income.data_source: Tracer survey and employer job-role verification

sdg_alignment.rationale: Reduce youth not in employment, education, or training
Decent work and equal pay opportunity
Economic participation and leadership opportunities for women
Income gains contribute to poverty reduction

data_collection_plan.method: spot checks, duplicate checks, source verification
